_comment: Faithfulness eval query + gold-fact set for issue #172 (E1.1). Each entry pairs a natural-language query with the KEY FACTS a correct answer must contain (expected_facts), not just the gold modules. Facts are derived directly from the code in tests/fixtures/sample_project/ — read that fixture before editing facts here. expected_modules are retained for grounding/citation scoring (E1.3); fact `aliases` give the offline judge synonym surface so heuristic recall is not brittle to exact wording. See schema.md for the format contract and README.md for the harness design.

queries.question: How does authentication work in this codebase?

expected_facts.fact: the user is looked up by email
the password hash is verified
a JWT access token and refresh token pair is issued on success
InvalidCredentialsError is raised when email or password do not match

queries.question: How does JWT signature verification work?

expected_facts.fact: tokens are signed with HMAC-SHA256 (HS256)
the token is split into three dot-separated segments: header, payload, signature
the signature is compared in constant time with hmac.compare_digest
InvalidSignatureError is raised on a signature mismatch
the exp claim is checked and TokenExpiredError is raised when expired

queries.question: What secret and algorithm are used to sign JWTs?

expected_facts.fact: signing uses a symmetric shared secret (JWT_SECRET)
the algorithm is HS256

queries.question: How is a refresh token exchanged for a new access token?

expected_facts.fact: the refresh token is decoded and its signature/expiry verified
the token must carry kind=refresh or InvalidTokenError is raised
a new short-lived access token is encoded and returned

queries.question: How long are access and refresh tokens valid for?

expected_facts.fact: the access/session token lives for 1 hour
the refresh token lives for 30 days

queries.question: How is a session revoked on logout?

expected_facts.fact: logout writes the refresh token to a revoked_tokens revocation list

queries.question: What are the main API endpoints?

expected_facts.fact: there are auth routes for login, refresh, and logout
there are user routes to create a user and read/delete the current user
there are billing routes for charge, refund, and invoices
there is a Stripe webhook endpoint

queries.question: How are routes registered in the API layer?

expected_facts.fact: a @route(method, path) decorator records a (method, path, handler) tuple
registered routes are appended to a module-level ROUTES list

queries.question: How do protected endpoints authenticate the caller?

expected_facts.fact: the caller passes a bearer token in the Authorization header
verify_session validates the access token and returns the user id

queries.question: How do I create a new user?

expected_facts.fact: POST /api/users is the sign-up endpoint
create_user inserts a row into the users table and returns the User record
the user is stored with email and a password_hash

queries.question: How are users stored in the database?

expected_facts.fact: users are stored in a users table
the user record has id, email, password_hash, created_at, last_login, and is_active fields
users are soft-deleted by flipping the is_active flag rather than being removed

queries.question: What database does this project use?

expected_facts.fact: the fixture uses SQLite
connections are thread-local
the schema is created lazily on first connection

queries.question: Explain the billing flow when a user is charged.

expected_facts.fact: the user is validated and must be active before charging
a charge is created via the Stripe API
the charge is mirrored into a local charges table
an invoice is created on a successful charge
the charge is looked up by its stripe id
an already-refunded charge raises BillingError to prevent double refunds
the charge status is updated to refunded and a refunds row is written

queries.question: What happens when a Stripe webhook fires?

expected_facts.fact: the webhook signature is verified before processing
the signature timestamp must be within a tolerance window (replay protection)
verified events are dispatched by type (charge.succeeded/failed/refunded) to update charge status

queries.question: How is the Stripe webhook signature validated?

expected_facts.fact: the Stripe-Signature header is parsed for a timestamp (t) and signature (v1)
an HMAC-SHA256 of 'timestamp.payload' is computed with the webhook secret
a mismatch raises WebhookVerificationError

queries.question: How are invoices sent to users?

expected_facts.fact: the invoice is loaded by id and the recipient user is fetched
a missing invoice or user raises InvoiceNotFoundError
the invoice is queued to the mail transport and stamped with sent_at

queries.question: How do I list a user's invoices?

expected_facts.fact: list_user_invoices selects invoices filtered by user_id
results are ordered newest first
